clinicalProblem.clinicalIntent: Identify and exclude white-coat effect in medicated adults with elevated office BP but without severe hypertension or resistance
clinicalProblem.whenToUse: Adults on antihypertensive medication with office SBP ≥130 or DBP ≥80, but excluding those with resistant hypertension or SBP ≥160 or DBP ≥100
clinicalProblem.decisionOrAction: Recommend out-of-office BP monitoring to exclude white-coat effect

navigationPath.purpose: Confirm patient is taking antihypertensive medication
Enter office SBP and DBP readings
Rule out resistant hypertension and severe BP (SBP ≥160 or DBP ≥100)
Guide patient on ambulatory or home BP monitoring
Determine if white-coat effect is present

rules: Verify patient meets all inclusion criteria
Verify patient meets no exclusion criteria
If criteria met → recommend out-of-office BP monitoring
Purpose: Exclude white-coat effect (elevated office BP but normal out-of-office BP)
Use ambulatory BP monitoring (ABPM) or home BP monitoring (HBPM)
Compare office readings with out-of-office averages
White-coat effect confirmed if out-of-office BP is normal (<130/80)

thresholds.sbp: N/A - white-coat exclusion is not a crisis protocol

appDisplay.behavior: Normal routing - proceed with out-of-office monitoring

nextStepRouting.providerSuggestion: Order ambulatory BP monitoring or provide home BP monitor
nextStepRouting.followUpTiming: Repeat out-of-office monitoring in 2-4 weeks if white-coat effect suspected
nextStepRouting.repeatMeasurement: Confirm with multiple out-of-office readings (3-7 days of HBPM or 24-hour ABPM)
nextStepRouting.navigatorEscalation: Flag if out-of-office BP confirms sustained hypertension requiring medication adjustment
nextStepRouting.documentation: Log COR 2a, LOE B-NR recommendation and monitoring results
